title: Praven Prime

{{Quote|Recitation: For example, on Praven Prime, the simple transferring of L'Xing syntax for 'friendship' changes its meaning—and implies that one's brood mate was actually impregnated by their own host. ''[…]'' Statement: This comment, of course, caused a civil war between the Gu-vandi Collective and L'Xing that still persists to the current date.|HK-47|<ref name="KOTOR2" />}}
'''Praven Prime''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] in the [[Quelii sector/Legends|Quelii sector]] that was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the [[L'Xing]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. By [[3951 BBY]], it was a part of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as well as situated in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] controlled by the [[Gu-vandi]] species. However, an intentional mistranslation of a [[L'Xing (language)|L'Xing]] [[Language/Legends|language]] phrase by an [[HK-50 series assassin droid]] masquerading as a [[Translator droid/Legends|translator droid]] led to a [[Gu-vandi Collective–L'Xing civil war|civil war]] between the L'Xing and the [[Gu-vandi Collective]]. The conflict eventually resulted in the Republic withdrawing from both Praven Prime and Gu-vandi space.

==Description==
Praven Prime was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="KOTOR2" /> located in the [[Praven Prime system/Legends|Praven Prime system]], a part of the [[Quelii sector/Legends|Quelii sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" />

==History==
{{Quote|Answer: In the case of Praven Prime, the civil war actually forced the Republic to back out of Gu-vandi space and let their world fall from Republic control. As I understand it, that would be best.|HK-47|<ref name="KOTOR2" />}}
[[File:Hk50full.jpg|thumb|left|175px|An HK-50 series assassin droid (a unit pictured) caused a civil war involving Praven Prime's L'Xing species.]]
By [[3959 BBY]], Praven Prime was a part of the "civilized [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]," the territory that had been widely [[Scout/Legends|explored]] by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="TEA" /> By some point [[3951 BBY|eight years]] later,<ref name="Dates">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', which establishes that the [[Gu-vandi Collective–L'Xing civil war|civil war]] between the [[Gu-vandi Collective]] and the [[L'Xing]] broke out no more than four [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior, to [[3951 BBY]].</ref> the world was situated in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] controlled by the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Gu-vandi]] [[Species/Legends|species]] and was also a member of the Republic.<ref name="KOTOR2" />

An [[HK-50 series assassin droid]]<ref name="KOTOR2" />—a unit of an [[HK-series assassin droid|assassin droid model]] that had originally been designed by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan]] and that was later [[HK Manufacturing Plant|mass-produced]] by the rogue [[G0-T0 infrastructure planning system]] known as [[G0-T0]]<ref name="KOTORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>—masquerading as a [[Translator droid/Legends|translator droid]] at one point<ref name="KOTOR2" /> between [[3955 BBY]] and 3951 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]]<ref name="Dates" /> intentionally mistranslated the word for "friendship" in the [[L'Xing (language)|L'Xing]] [[Language/Legends|language]]—spoken by Praven Prime's [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] species of the [[L'Xing|same name]]—to instead convey the implication that a specific individual's [[Reproduction/Legends|brood]] mate had been impregnated by their own host. The translated comment sparked a [[Gu-vandi Collective–L'Xing civil war|civil war]] between the L'Xing and the [[Gu-vandi Collective]]. As a result of the civil war, the Republic decided to relinquish control over Praven Prime and withdrew from Gu'vandi space.<ref name="KOTOR2" />

{{GameLSmechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords}}
[[File:HK-47.png|thumb|right|180px|HK-47 (pictured) claimed the civil war would have a positive impact on Praven Prime's inhabitants.]]
In 3951 BBY,<ref name="Dates" /> while the civil war was still ongoing, [[HK-47]], an [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]]<ref name="KOTOR2" /> who had originally served as the basis for the HK-50 model,<ref name="KOTORCG" /> informed the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] known as the [[Meetra Surik|Jedi Exile]] about the role of Praven Prime in the eruption of the civil war. The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] reasoned that the outbreak of the conflict and the withdrawal of the Republic would eventually prove to be beneficial to that government, speculating that continued control over the world would have been a "token gesture of control" as well as an unnecessary burden in the form of resources needed to maintain [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] and [[Merchant/Legends|trade]] relations. HK-47 further claimed that the drastic reduction of the L'Xing population caused by the war would let the Republic eventually supply them with emergency relief at a substantially decreased cost and also that the violence involved had "woken" both the L'Xing and the Gu-vandi from their relatively inactive dispositions.<ref name="KOTOR2" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|Besides, master, quite frankly, the Gu-vandi and L'Xing needed a good war. They were races that relied more on words than actions, and a good, brisk killing woke them from their torpid state.|HK-47, to the Jedi Exile|audio=HK-47TheirTorpidState.flac|<ref name="KOTOR2" />}}
Praven Prime was home to the sentient L'Xing species, members of which spoke a language of the same name.<ref name="KOTOR2" />

==Behind the scenes==
Praven Prime was introduced in the [[2004]] [[Obsidian Entertainment]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''. In the game, the world can be mentioned in an optional conversation the player character, the Jedi Exile, can have with the character HK-47. This article assumes one hundred percent game completion and that the conversation took place as described.<ref name="KOTOR2" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Praven Prime [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the world itself, in grid square O-6.<ref name="TEA" />
